page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16-lssd is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel Xeon 6985P-C cores on the x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz and features a large 504 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 124 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core. It also bundles 539 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across two drives, though it does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, reaching 366.16 tokens per second with a 135M parameter model. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range, supported by advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-16-lssd offers a balanced profile for workloads requiring high memory density and fast local storage without the premium of dedicated GPU hardware. The combination of dedicated Intel Xeon cores, a substantial L3 cache, and local NVMe SSDs makes this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, text processing pipelines, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16-lssd is a memory-optimized instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 124 GB of RAM, and 539 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture and a 504 MB L3 cache, this server delivers strong single-core performance. Benchmarks highlight top-tier capabilities in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and text processing, alongside high-speed text generation for small language models. While multi-core and database benchmarks remain in the average tier, the instance's high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage provide a cost-effective platform for memory-heavy applications. It is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, text processing, and lightweight inference workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16-lssd features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 124 GB RAM, and 539 GB local NVMe SSD. Powered by Intel Xeon processors, it is optimized for memory-intensive databases and text processing.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-capacity RAM, and fast local NVMe SSD storage for demanding data workloads.
